hypothesis: Incorporating explicit electronegativity difference tensors between shell 1 and shell 2 elements as an additional conditioning signal in the latent diffusion model will improve adsorption energy prediction accuracy and generation fidelity for multi-element catalytic surfaces, because charge transfer effects across shell boundaries—which are captured by inter-shell electronegativity gradients but absent from the current composition-only representation—are a primary determinant of adsorbate binding strength in high-entropy alloy systems.
